Off Calendar Feb-03-2022 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-19-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Reconsideration Of Judgment Dismissing Proceedings As To State Bar Of California And Mayte Diaz And Served On Defendant On November 22, 2021 (Ccp 1008 & Ccp 1012 (A); Time To Move For Reconsideration Is 10 Days From Service Of Order Or Judgment And Additional 5 Days If Service By Us Mail)
Off Calendar Jan-31-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
DANIEL EVERETT, (415) 891-9472, Plaintiff in Pro Per, (In-person appearance)
JAMES J. CHANG, ESQ., OFFICE OF GENERAL COUNSEL THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, (415) 538-2381, james.chang@calbar.ca.gov, for Defendants State Bar of California; Mayte Diaz,

Motion heard by Judge Etahn P. Schulman at 1:30 p.m.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DANIEL EVERETT Motion For Reconsideration Of Judgment Dismissing Proceedings As To State Bar Of California And Mayte Diaz And Served On Defendant On November 22, 2021 (Ccp 1008 & Ccp 1012 (A); Time To Move For Reconsideration Is 10 Days From Service Of Order Or Judgment And Additional 5 Days If Service By Us Mail)

Plaintiff Daniel Everett's motion for reconsideration is denied. By order filed November 1, 2021, the Court sustained Defendants State Bar of California and Mayte Diaz's demurrer to Plaintiff's complaint without leave to amend on the ground that Plaintiff failed to serve or file any opposition to the demurrer. Defendant State Bar served notice of entry of the Court's order by mail on Plaintiff at his address of record on November 2, 2021. The Court subsequently entered judgment in favor of Defendants. On December 7, 2021, Plaintiff filed the instant motion for reconsideration of the Court's judgment. Although Plaintiff asserts that he was not properly served with the Court's order, he acknowledges that it was served on him by mail at "the service address related to this case." (Everett Decl. 2.) Further, he does not assert in his declaration that he was not properly served with Defendants' demurrer, nor does he offer any explanation for his failure to file opposition to the demurrer. Finally, he offers no grounds for reconsideration of the Court's judgment.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2021 Continued To Feb-16-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, MAYTE DIAZ' Demurrer to Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Defendants filed their demurrer on August 27, 2021 and served it on Plaintiff by first class mail on the same date. Plaintiff failed to serve and file any opposition to the demurrer. His one-page hand-scrawled "preliminary response" dated October 25, 2021-the same day the State Bar filed a notice of non-opposition by Plaintiff-appears to assert that Plaintiff timely submitted an opposition for filing; however, no such opposition has been received by the Court for filing, nor has Plaintiff provided this department with the courtesy copies required by the Local Rules. (See SF Local Rule 2.7(B).) Nor did Plaintiff serve any opposition on the State Bar.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
